quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungarian paprika isn't just one spice — there are eight official grades ranging from delicate and sweet to fiery hot! The town of Szeged is so famous for paprika production that it has an entire museum dedicated to this beloved red spice, which became Hungary's national symbol.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals can hold their breath for up to 30 minutes and dive nearly 1,500 feet deep. Learn about these charming marine mammals that call the Strait of Juan de Fuca home.

medium_read.title: How Plants Know When It's Time to Bloom
medium_read.teaser: Plants have an internal clock that responds to daylight, temperature, and even memory of past seasons. Discover the fascinating science behind how your garden knows exactly when spring has arrived.

long_read.title: The Women Who Mapped the Ocean Floor
long_read.teaser: Marie Tharp's beautiful hand-drawn maps revealed underwater mountains, valleys, and the Mid-Atlantic Ridge, changing our understanding of Earth forever. Her story is one of persistence, artistry, and scientific brilliance in an era when women scientists faced enormous barriers.

port_angeles.title: Roosevelt Elk Viewing Season
port_angeles.description: July is a wonderful time to spot Roosevelt elk in the Hoh Rain Forest and nearby meadows. These majestic animals, named after President Theodore Roosevelt, are the largest of the four elk subspecies in North America. Early morning or dusk offers the best viewing opportunities along Hurricane Ridge Road.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while in flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That tiny heart is working incredibly hard to power those amazing wings!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 1,200 beats per minute — that's about 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts are remarkable — a hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute in flight!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To fuel this incredible metabolism, hummingbirds must eat approximately every 10-15 minutes and consume up to half their body weight in sugar daily. At night, they enter a hibernation-like state called torpor to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'hello' or 'goodbye'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Szia (pronounced 'see-ya') is the casual, friendly way to say both hello and goodbye!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: It's 'szia' (pronounced 'see-ya') — such a friendly word that works for both hello and goodbye!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: For a more formal greeting, Hungarians say 'jó napot' (good day). The informal 'szia' actually comes from the word 'szolga' meaning servant, similar to how 'goodbye' in English comes from 'God be with you.'

puzzle_3.question: What do black bears in the Olympic Peninsula primarily eat?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes! Despite their fierce reputation, Olympic black bears are mostly vegetarian, dining on berries, grasses, and plants for about 90% of their diet.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Surprisingly, black bears here are mostly vegetarian! About 90% of their diet is berries, grasses, and plants, though they do occasionally eat insects and small animals.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Olympic black bears particularly love huckleberries, salmonberries, and salal berries in summer. They need to consume up to 20,000 calories per day in fall to prepare for winter, when they enter their dens for a long rest.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
